sourceMovie.title: How to Have Sex
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit How to Have Sex for the same reason it broke through in 2023: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Molly Manning Walker keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Lady Bird
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Have Sex clicked for you, Lady Bird (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Atonement
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Have Sex clicked for you, Atonement (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Have Sex?
faq.answer: Try Lady Bird, Atonement, The Edge of Seventeen—each shares DNA with How to Have Sex while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Have Sex?
faq.answer: How to Have Sex (2023) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Have Sex worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
